       The frontend lives in /src (*.ts) and is compiled to /wwwroot/dist by the
       `npm run build` script (tsc + scripts/copy-assets.mjs). Since wwwroot/dist is
       gitignored, it must be (re)generated before the C# build so the static files
       are present for `UseStaticFiles`/publish. These targets wire npm into the
       MSBuild pipeline so `dotnet build` and `dotnet publish` are self-contained.

       NpmInstall is incremental: it only runs when package.json or package-lock.json
       change (or node_modules is absent). node_modules/.package-lock.json is npm's
       own stamp file and serves as the output/up-to-date marker. -->
  <Target Name="NpmInstall"
          Inputs="package.json;package-lock.json"
          Outputs="node_modules/.package-lock.json">
    <Exec Command="npm install" WorkingDirectory="$(MSBuildProjectDirectory)" />
  </Target>

  <!-- NpmBuild always runs (tsc is fast and honors noEmitOnError), but only after
       deps are in place. Runs before BeforeBuild so dist exists prior to CoreBuild
       and static-asset copy during publish. -->
  <Target Name="NpmBuild"
          DependsOnTargets="NpmInstall"
          BeforeTargets="BeforeBuild">
    <Exec Command="npm run build" WorkingDirectory="$(MSBuildProjectDirectory)" />
  </Target>

    // =====================================================================
    // Exception-translation consolidation contract.
    //
    // The Execute/ExecuteAsync helpers translate exactly three "root"
    // exception types — ArgumentException, UnauthorizedAccessException and
    // IOException — into a 400, and let everything else propagate. The
    // DirectoryNotFound/FileNotFound/PathTooLong catches that used to be
    // listed explicitly are redundant because all three derive from
    // IOException; a single IOException branch handles them by inheritance.
    // The tests below pin that inheritance contract for *every* translated
    // root (including non-BCL derived types) and that the catch list is not
    // accidentally widened to swallow unrelated exceptions.
    // =====================================================================

    [Theory]
    [MemberData(nameof(IOExceptionBranchMembers))]
    public void Translate_CatchesEveryIOExceptionSubtype_AsBadRequest400(Exception ex)
    {
        // The consolidated IOException branch must catch the base type AND
        // every derived subtype — including the ones previously enumerated by
        // name (DirectoryNotFound/FileNotFound/PathTooLong) and ones that were
        // never named (EndOfStream/FileLoad), plus a non-BCL subclass. If the
        // branch were rewritten as exact-type matching (`ex.GetType() ==
        // typeof(IOException)`), the subtype cases here would propagate and
        // this assertion would fail.
        var fake = new FakeFileService { DeleteException = ex };
        var controller = CreateController(fake);

        var result = controller.Delete("any");

        var badRequest = Assert.IsType<BadRequestObjectResult>(result);
        Assert.Equal(StatusCodes.Status400BadRequest, badRequest.StatusCode);
        Assert.Equal(ex.Message, GetProperty(badRequest.Value!, "error"));
    }

    public static IEnumerable<object[]> IOExceptionBranchMembers()
    {
        yield return new object[] { new IOException("io") };
        yield return new object[] { new DirectoryNotFoundException("dne") };
        yield return new object[] { new FileNotFoundException("fnf") };
        yield return new object[] { new PathTooLongException("ptl") };
        yield return new object[] { new EndOfStreamException("eos") };
        yield return new object[] { new FileLoadException("fl") };
        yield return new object[] { new CustomIOException("custom-io") };
    }

    [Theory]

    public void Translate_CatchesEveryArgumentExceptionSubtype_AsBadRequest400(Exception ex)
    {
        // Symmetric to the IOException case: the ArgumentException branch must
        // catch the base type and every derived subtype, including a non-BCL
        // subclass, by inheritance — never by exact-type matching.
        var fake = new FakeFileService { DeleteException = ex };
        var controller = CreateController(fake);

        var result = controller.Delete("any");

        var badRequest = Assert.IsType<BadRequestObjectResult>(result);
        Assert.Equal(StatusCodes.Status400BadRequest, badRequest.StatusCode);
        Assert.Equal(ex.Message, GetProperty(badRequest.Value!, "error"));
    }

    public static IEnumerable<object[]> ArgumentExceptionBranchMembers()
    {
        yield return new object[] { new ArgumentException("arg") };
        yield return new object[] { new ArgumentNullException("p", "argnull") };
        yield return new object[] { new ArgumentOutOfRangeException("p", "argoor") };
        yield return new object[] { new CustomArgumentException("custom-arg") };
    }

    [MemberData(nameof(NonTranslatedExceptions))]

        var fake = new FakeFileService { DeleteException = ex };

    /// and <see cref="IOException"/> — and relies on inheritance to also catch

    /// <see cref="FileNotFoundException"/>, <see cref="PathTooLongException"/>,
    /// <see cref="EndOfStreamException"/> and <see cref="FileLoadException"/> are

    /// catches them all (the former two used to be enumerated explicitly but
    /// are redundant); <see cref="ArgumentNullException"/> and
    /// <see cref="ArgumentOutOfRangeException"/> are <see cref="ArgumentException"/>
    /// subtypes. These cases pin that the inherited-subtype contract holds for
    /// every translated root, not just the named ones.
    /// </summary>
    public static IEnumerable<object[]> CaughtExceptions()
    {
        // The three root types the controller documents and catches. Each must
        // map to a 400 carrying its Message.
        yield return new object[] { new ArgumentException("arg-message") };
        yield return new object[] { new UnauthorizedAccessException("unauth-message") };
        yield return new object[] { new IOException("io-message") };

        // IOException subtypes that must be caught by virtue of inheritance.

        // subtype must ALSO resolve to 400; exact-type matching would let these
        // leak through as unhandled. This pins the consolidation that folds the

        yield return new object[] { new DirectoryNotFoundException("dir-message") };

        // ArgumentException subtypes, same inheritance contract.
        yield return new object[] { new ArgumentNullException("p", "argnull-message") };
        yield return new object[] { new ArgumentOutOfRangeException("p", "argoor-message") };

    // =====================================================================
    // Exception-translation consolidation contract.
    //
    // Execute (sync) and ExecuteAsync (async) translate exactly three "root"
    // exception types into a 400 — ArgumentException, UnauthorizedAccessException
    // and IOException — and let every other type propagate. The former explicit
    // DirectoryNotFound/FileNotFound catches are redundant (both are IOException
    // subtypes); a single IOException branch handles them by inheritance. The
    // tests below pin the inheritance contract for non-BCL derived types, the
    // precise catch-list boundary (no widening), and parity between the sync
    // and async code paths after they are folded onto one shared translator.
    // =====================================================================

    [Theory]
    [MemberData(nameof(CustomTranslatedSubclasses))]
    public void Translate_CatchesNonBclSubclasses_ByInheritance(Exception ex)
    {
        // The translation branches must match by inheritance (is-a), not by
        // exact type: a non-BCL exception derived from IOException or
        // ArgumentException must still resolve to 400. Exact-type matching
        // (`ex.GetType() == typeof(IOException)`) would let these propagate.
        var fake = new FakeFileService { DeleteException = ex };
        var controller = CreateController(fake);

        var result = controller.Delete("any");

        var badRequest = Assert.IsType<BadRequestObjectResult>(result);
        Assert.Equal(StatusCodes.Status400BadRequest, badRequest.StatusCode);
        Assert.Equal(ex.Message, GetProperty(badRequest.Value!, "error"));
    }

    public static IEnumerable<object[]> CustomTranslatedSubclasses()

        yield return new object[] { new CustomIOException("custom-io") };
        yield return new object[] { new CustomArgumentException("custom-arg") };

        // `catch (Exception ...)` that forgets to re-throw, or catching a common
        // base like SystemException — would silently turn these into 400s and
        // hide real faults. Each must surface as the exact same instance thrown
        // by the service, unwrapped.
        var fake = new FakeFileService { DeleteException = ex };
        var controller = CreateController(fake);

        var thrown = Record.Exception(() => controller.Delete("any"));

        Assert.Same(ex, thrown);

The baseline passes (397 tests). Now let me analyze the refactor's risk areas before writing strengthening tests.
